else:\\n        total_steps = max(1, steps_per_epoch * 100)\\n\\n    warmup_steps = max(1, int(0.1 * total_steps))\\n\\n    def lr_lambda(step_idx: int) -> float:\\n        if step_idx < warmup_steps:\\n            return float(step_idx + 1) / float(warmup_steps)\\n        progress = (step_idx - warmup_steps) / float(max(1, total_steps - warmup_steps))\\n        return 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))\\n\\n    return optim.lr_scheduler.LambdaLR(optimizer, lr_lambda=lr_lambda)\",\"text\":\"\"}],\"primary_mode\":\"summaries\",\"rationale\":\"With only 256 fixed training steps, the current scheduler is effectively inactive. This change makes LR scheduling budget-aware and adds a short warmup plus cosine decay over the actual step budget to improve early optimization efficiency without increasing complexity much.\",\"secondary_modes\":[\"topk\"]}"}}
